design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from four Avengers, progress through side-scrolling levels fighting waves of themed enemies and supervillains, interspersed with flying/shooting sequences; defeat bosses tied to Marvel villain roster
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing melee combat positioning against multiple enemies while deploying character-specific ranged special attacks (Avenger Attack); switching between ground and aerial combat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic Marvel Avengers (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision) utilizing their signature powers to battle Red Skull's assembled supervillain army
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Character-role differentiation: each Avenger has distinct attack patterns (projectile vs. energy beam) and movement mechanics (flying kick vs. angled beam during jumps). Heavy emphasis on Marvel IP authenticity with boss roster drawn directly from comic continuity rather than original antagonists

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides tactical variation; different Avengers have distinct combat ranges and special attack properties affecting difficulty approach

progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss progression gates access to subsequent levels; defeat Red Skull's villain army in sequence (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, Controller, Crossbones, Red Skull)
replayability_hooks: Four distinct playable characters with different attack mechanics encourage multiple playthroughs
Multiplayer simultaneous play (Genesis version supported co-op)
Different versions across platforms offer varying adaptations

world_building_techniques: Boss encounters structured around authentic Marvel villain roster, reinforcing comic book narrative
Environmental level design implicitly tied to villain themes (e.g., Mandarin stage, Ultron stage)
Supporting Avengers appear as helpers (Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor), expanding team presence

boss_design.philosophy: Showcase Marvel supervillain roster as climactic encounters; each boss represents a named antagonist from Avengers comics with thematic level contexts

faction_indicators: Red Skull as authoritarian villain assembling superpower army for global conquest
Avengers as multinational defense coalition (American, billionaire tech, archer, synthetic being)
danger_signals: Assembled supervillain army representing concentrated threat
environmental_storytelling: Level progression through villain-themed stages suggests infiltration of villain infrastructure
Flying sequences represent breach of air defenses or villain bases
Mandarin stage implies Eastern/foreign threat dimension

sources_used: wikipedia: Captain America and the Avengers
